title: Foundation / Makeup on Silk
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; cold blot only at home
stainChemistry: Liquid foundation = pigment (iron oxide / titanium dioxide) + oils + silicone (dimethicone) + emulsifiers + sometimes polymer film former. Silicone is the difference vs lipstick — it's hydrophobic AND slick, so most household surfactants can't touch it. Silicone-based foundations need a silicone-targeting solvent. On silk, the cotton makeup-removal toolkit (Dawn + warm water + scrubbing) doesn't address silicone AND damages silk. Pro uses cold POG / petroleum ether + cold NSD + (occasionally) sodium hydrosulfite for residual color.
whyThisWorks: Cold solvent (silicone-compatible) penetrates the silicone phase, releases oil and pigment, NSD lifts dissolved residue. Reducing bleach for any residual D&C dye color. Home protocol = lift bulk, cold blot, pro within hours.
defaultAssumption: Silk + foundation = pro within hours. Most modern foundations contain silicone — home soap won't touch it. Tinted moisturizers (less polymer / silicone) are slightly easier.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Use the flat edge of a plastic card to lift surface excess. Wipe card on cloth between passes. No smearing or rubbing.
Cold water through the BACK over a catch towel. Removes water-miscible fraction; silicone-based foundations resist this but it's worth attempting.
Cold solvent penetrates the silicone matrix. Tight blotting from edge inward. Refresh cloth each pass.
Cold NSD lifts dissolved pigment + emulsifier. Press only.
For residual D&C dye color. Strict 60-sec dwell, silk-safer than peroxide.
Cold final rinse. Press between dry towels, lay flat to air-dry.

steps: 1. Plastic card flat-edge lift surface excess.
3. Cold POG / petroleum ether for silicone + oil.
4. Cold NSD sponge press for pigment.
5. Sodium hydrosulfite for residual dye color, 60-sec ceiling.
6. Cold final rinse, press dry flat.
warnings: NEVER isopropyl alcohol on silk — strips dyes
NEVER acetone on silk — destroys fibroin
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes

homeSolutions.instruction: Foundation on silk is harder than lipstick because of silicone. Most modern foundations contain dimethicone (silicone), which is hydrophobic AND slick — household soap can't touch it. Add silk's banned-tools list (no alcohol, no acetone, no peroxide, no OxiClean), and home options shrink to bulk-lift + cold-water back-flush. Pro chemistry has silicone-compatible solvents that home doesn't.

homeSolutions.agent: Lift Surface Excess — Plastic Card Edge
homeSolutions.instruction: Use the FLAT edge of a plastic card to lift any surface excess. Don't scrape or rub. Wipe the card on a separate cloth between passes.

Turn inside out. Place white towel under stain. Run COLD water through the BACK into the catch towel. Cold ONLY. The water-miscible fraction (water + emulsifier) lifts; the silicone fraction stays. That's expected — pro handles the silicone.

homeSolutions.agent: Press Dry — DO NOT Apply Anything Else
homeSolutions.instruction: Press silk between two dry white towels. No wringing. Lay flat at room temperature, out of sun. Do NOT apply rubbing alcohol (strips silk dyes), acetone, peroxide, OxiClean, or Dawn (Dawn won't touch silicone but WILL drive pigment in if you rub it). Silk's best outcome is reaching the pro with nothing applied.

CALL FIRST — "Do you treat foundation on silk with cold POG?" If they don't know what POG is, try another cleaner. Tell them: "Foundation on silk, [silicone-based / water-based / tinted moisturizer], age [hours]. Plastic-card lift + cold-water back-flush ONLY at home. No solvent, no detergent applied."

steps: 2. Plastic card flat-edge lift of surface excess.
3. Cold water back-flush from the reverse, into catch towel.
4. Press dry between white towels — no other chemistry.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after the cold-water back-flush. Silicone-based foundations don't respond to home soap, and the household lipstick toolkit damages silk.
warnings: NEVER alcohol / IPA on silk — strips dyes
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes
NEVER rub foundation in — drives pigment + silicone deeper
Pour through, not on top — water rings

fiberSensitivities: Silk fibroin damaged by acetone + most aggressive solvents
Silk dyes stripped by isopropyl alcohol
Silicone-based foundations need pro silicone solvent

materialWarnings: Pro within hours — silicone foundations require pro solvent
Don't rub — drives pigment + silicone deeper

escalation.when: Always — silk + foundation is pro-lane within hours, especially silicone-based foundations.
escalation.whatToTell: "Foundation on silk, [silicone-based / water-based / tinted moisturizer if known], age [hours]. Plastic-card lift + cold-water back-flush ONLY at home. No alcohol, no acetone, no detergent, no peroxide applied."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in silk (call ahead — confirm cold POG / silicone-compatible solvent capability)

contextQuestions: Foundation type — silicone-based / water-based / oil-based / tinted moisturizer?
Has anyone applied alcohol / Dawn / acetone / micellar water?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Silicone-based foundations are the hardest variant. Tinted moisturizers (less polymer) easier. Long-wear foundations have additional film former — even harder. Pre-applied alcohol = silk dye stripped where applied; pre-applied micellar water rings silk.
